allow:
Bash(gh issue create --title "Foundation: Docker Compose + Project Skeleton + Unified Response" --body "$\(cat <<'EOF'
## What to build

Set up the complete development foundation so all subsequent vertical slices can be built and tested end-to-end.

**End-to-end behavior**: A developer can run `docker-compose up`, start the Spring Boot application, access Swagger UI at `/doc.html`, and see a properly structured API response with unified error handling.

**What this includes**:
- Docker Compose with MySQL 8.x, Redis 7.x, RabbitMQ 3.x \(with management plugin\), Elasticsearch 8.x
- Maven multi-module project structure \(smart-health-common, smart-health-user, smart-health-registration, smart-health-consultation, smart-health-prescription, smart-health-app\)
- Database schema creation scripts for all core tables \(t_patient, t_doctor_schedule, t_registration_order, t_prescription, t_pharmacy_inventory\)
- Elasticsearch index mapping for medical knowledge \(idx_medical_knowledge with ik analyzer + dense_vector 1536-dim\)
- Unified API response format: `{code, message, data}`
- Global exception handler returning structured error responses
- SpringDoc + Knife4j integration with Swagger UI accessible
- Basic health check endpoint

**What this does NOT include**: Any business logic, authentication, or feature implementation.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `docker-compose up` starts all middleware successfully
- [ ] Maven project compiles with all modules
- [ ] Database tables created via migration scripts
- [ ] ES index created with correct mapping
- [ ] GET `/api/v1/health` returns `{"code": 200, "message": "success", "data": "ok"}`
- [ ] GET `/api/v1/nonexistent` returns `{"code": 404, "message": "Resource not found", "data": null}`
- [ ] Swagger UI accessible at `/doc.html` showing the health endpoint

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately
EOF
\)" 2>&1)

Bash(gh issue create --title "User Registration & JWT Authentication" --body "$\(cat <<'EOF'
## What to build

Complete user authentication flow so patients can register, login, and access protected APIs with JWT tokens.

**End-to-end behavior**: A patient registers with a phone number, logs in with credentials, receives a JWT token, and uses that token to access a protected API endpoint successfully.

**What this includes**:
- Patient registration endpoint \(phone number + password\)
- Password hashing with bcrypt
- Login endpoint returning JWT token
- JWT token generation with patient ID and expiration
- Spring Security configuration with JWT filter
- Protected API endpoint demonstration
- Token validation and refresh logic
- Proper error responses for invalid credentials, expired tokens, etc.

**What this does NOT include**: Role-based authorization, admin users, or doctor accounts \(out of scope for phase 1\).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] POST `/api/v1/auth/register` creates a patient account with phone + password
- [ ] POST `/api/v1/auth/login` returns JWT token for valid credentials
- [ ] JWT token contains patient ID and expiration time
- [ ] Protected endpoint returns 401 without token
- [ ] Protected endpoint returns 200 with valid token
- [ ] Protected endpoint returns 401 with expired/invalid token
- [ ] Password stored as bcrypt hash in database
- [ ] Duplicate phone number registration returns appropriate error

## Blocked by

- #1 \(Foundation: Docker Compose + Project Skeleton + Unified Response\)
EOF
\)" 2>&1)

Bash(gh issue create --title "Doctor Schedule Management" --body "$\(cat <<'EOF'
## What to build

Doctor schedule management so admins can create schedules and patients can view available appointment slots.

**End-to-end behavior**: An admin creates a doctor schedule with date, shift, quota, and fee. A patient queries available schedules and sees the list with remaining slots.

**What this includes**:
- Schedule creation endpoint \(admin\) with doctor info, date, shift \(morning/afternoon\), quota, fee
- Schedule query endpoint \(patient\) returning available schedules with remaining slots
- Schedule table with optimistic locking \(version field\)
- Proper validation and error handling
- Schedule status management \(active/cancelled\)

**What this does NOT include**: Seckill logic, booking, or payment \(those come in the next slice\).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] POST `/api/v1/admin/schedule` creates a schedule with doctor, date, shift, quota, fee
- [ ] GET `/api/v1/schedules` returns list of available schedules with remaining slots
- [ ] Schedule includes doctor name, department, date, shift, fee, remaining quota
- [ ] Optimistic locking prevents concurrent modification conflicts
- [ ] Cannot create schedule with negative quota or invalid date
- [ ] Cancelled schedules are excluded from query results

## Blocked by

- #1 \(Foundation: Docker Compose + Project Skeleton + Unified Response\)
- #2 \(User Registration & JWT Authentication\)
EOF
\)" 2>&1)

Bash(gh issue create --title "Seckill Registration with Redis Pre-deduction" --body "$\(cat <<'EOF'
## What to build

High-concurrency registration seckill system so patients can grab scarce expert appointment slots without overselling.

**End-to-end behavior**: A patient clicks "grab appointment", the system pre-deducts inventory in Redis, acquires a distributed lock, sends a message to RabbitMQ, and asynchronously creates a registration order. The patient can check their order status \(queuing/pending payment/paid/completed\).

**What this includes**:
- Seckill endpoint for patients to grab an appointment slot
- Redis pre-deduction of inventory \(decrement quota in Redis\)
- Redisson distributed lock to prevent overselling under concurrency
- RabbitMQ message sent after successful Redis deduction
- Async consumer creates registration order in database
- Order number generation: `REG_yyyyMMdd_sequence` \(globally unique\)
- Order status tracking: QUEUING → PENDING_PAYMENT → PAID → COMPLETED
- Order query endpoint for patients
- Idempotency: same patient cannot grab same schedule twice

**What this does NOT include**: Payment integration \(mock only\), order cancellation, or refund logic.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] POST `/api/v1/registration/seckill` accepts schedule ID and returns order number
- [ ] Redis inventory pre-deducted atomically \(DECR with check\)
- [ ] Redisson distributed lock prevents concurrent overselling
- [ ] RabbitMQ message sent after successful Redis deduction
- [ ] Async consumer creates order in database with correct status
- [ ] Order number format: `REG_yyyyMMdd_XXXXXX` \(unique per day\)
- [ ] Same patient cannot grab same schedule twice \(idempotent check\)
- [ ] GET `/api/v1/registration/orders` returns patient's orders with status
- [ ] Under concurrent load \(100 threads\), inventory does not go negative
- [ ] Order status transitions correctly through the state machine

## Blocked by

- #3 \(Doctor Schedule Management\)
EOF
\)" 2>&1)

Bash(gh issue create --title "Multimodal Image Analysis" --body "$\(cat <<'EOF'
## What to build

Multimodal AI image analysis so patients can upload photos or medical reports and receive structured symptom analysis.

**End-to-end behavior**: A patient uploads a photo of a skin condition or medical report screenshot. The system calls a multimodal LLM \(via OpenAI Compatible API\), analyzes the image, and returns a structured symptom draft with possible conditions, severity assessment, and recommended next steps.

**What this includes**:
- Image upload endpoint \(multipart/form-data\) accepting JPG/PNG
- Image storage \(local filesystem for phase 1, OSS-ready interface\)
- Multimodal LLM integration via Spring AI \(OpenAI Compatible API\)
- Prompt engineering for medical image analysis
- Structured response parsing: symptoms, possible conditions, severity, recommendations
- Error handling for invalid images, API failures, timeout
- Rate limiting consideration \(prevent abuse\)

**What this does NOT include**: RAG retrieval, multi-turn conversation, or SSE streaming \(those come in the next slice\).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] POST `/api/v1/ai/multimodal/analyze` accepts multipart image upload
- [ ] Image validated for format \(JPG/PNG\) and size \(< 10MB\)
- [ ] Multimodal LLM called with image and medical analysis prompt
- [ ] Response parsed into structured format: symptoms, possible conditions, severity \(mild/moderate/severe\), recommendations
- [ ] API returns `{"code": 200, "data": {"symptoms": [...], "conditions": [...], "severity": "...", "recommendations": [...]}}`
- [ ] Invalid image format returns 400 error
- [ ] LLM API timeout returns 504 with user-friendly message
- [ ] Image stored locally with unique filename \(UUID-based\)

## Blocked by

- #2 \(User Registration & JWT Authentication\)
EOF
\)" 2>&1)

Bash(gh issue create --title "RAG Consultation with SSE Streaming" --body "$\(cat <<'EOF'
## What to build

RAG-powered AI consultation with SSE streaming so patients can have multi-turn conversations with an AI doctor that retrieves context from a medical knowledge base.

**End-to-end behavior**: A patient starts a consultation session, asks questions about their symptoms \(optionally referencing the multimodal analysis from the previous slice\). The system retrieves relevant medical knowledge from Elasticsearch \(text + vector search\), constructs a prompt with context, calls the LLM, and streams the response back via SSE \(typewriter effect\). The response includes citations to source documents. Multi-turn conversation context is maintained within the session.

**What this includes**:
- Session management: create session, list sessions, get session history
- Chat endpoint accepting user message + session ID
- RAG retrieval from Elasticsearch:
  - Text search with ik analyzer \(BM25\)
  - Vector search with dense_vector \(cosine similarity, 1536-dim embeddings\)
  - Hybrid retrieval combining both
- Prompt construction with retrieved context
- LLM call via Spring AI \(OpenAI Compatible API\)
- SSE streaming response \(Server-Sent Events\) with typewriter effect
- Citation extraction and inclusion in response
- Session context storage in MySQL \(JSON format in text field\)
- Conversation history maintained per session

**What this does NOT include**: Voice input, real-time video consultation, or doctor handoff \(out of scope for phase 1\).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] POST `/api/v1/ai/consult/stream` accepts message + session ID, returns SSE stream
- [ ] SSE events formatted correctly: `data: {"content": "...", "citations": [...]}\\n\\n`
- [ ] Response streamed token-by-token \(typewriter effect\)
- [ ] RAG retrieval returns top-K relevant documents from ES
- [ ] Citations included in response with source title and snippet
- [ ] Session created on first message, context maintained across turns
- [ ] GET `/api/v1/ai/sessions` returns patient's consultation sessions
- [ ] GET `/api/v1/ai/sessions/{id}/history` returns full conversation history
- [ ] LLM API failure returns error event in SSE stream
- [ ] Session context stored in MySQL \(JSON format\)

## Blocked by

- #5 \(Multimodal Image Analysis\)
EOF
\)" 2>&1)

Bash(gh issue create --title "Prescription Issuance with Inventory Deduction" --body "$\(cat <<'EOF'
## What to build

Electronic prescription system so doctors can issue prescriptions with diagnosis and medications, with real-time inventory deduction and PDF generation.

**End-to-end behavior**: A doctor views a patient's consultation records \(including AI-generated symptom drafts\), makes a diagnosis, and issues a prescription with a list of medications. The system deducts inventory from the pharmacy, generates a unique prescription code, and creates a PDF stub. The patient can view their prescription list and details.

**What this includes**:
- Prescription issuance endpoint \(doctor role\) with diagnosis + medication list
- Inventory deduction for each medication item \(pharmacy-specific\)
- Local transaction ensuring prescription creation + inventory deduction are atomic
- Prescription code generation: nationally unique format \(e.g., `RX_yyyyMMdd_hospitalId_sequence`\)
- PDF prescription stub generation \(stored locally, OSS-ready interface\)
- Prescription query endpoints: list for patient, details with medications
- Inventory query endpoint for pharmacy staff
- Validation: cannot prescribe if inventory insufficient

**What this does NOT include**: Pharmacist review workflow \(next slice\), payment integration, or drug interaction checking.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] POST `/api/v1/prescription/issue` accepts patient ID, consultation ID, diagnosis, medication list
- [ ] Inventory deducted atomically with prescription creation \(local transaction\)
- [ ] Insufficient inventory returns 400 error, no prescription created
- [ ] Prescription code generated: `RX_yyyyMMdd_hospitalId_XXXXXX` \(unique\)
- [ ] PDF stub generated and stored locally
- [ ] GET `/api/v1/prescriptions` returns patient's prescription list
- [ ] GET `/api/v1/prescriptions/{id}` returns prescription details with medications
- [ ] GET `/api/v1/pharmacy/inventory` returns inventory by pharmacy ID
- [ ] Transaction rollback on any failure \(no orphaned prescriptions or inventory deductions\)

## Blocked by

- #6 \(RAG Consultation with SSE Streaming\)
EOF
\)" 2>&1)

Bash(gh issue create --title "Prescription Review Workflow" --body "$\(cat <<'EOF'
## What to build

Prescription review workflow so pharmacists can approve or reject prescriptions to ensure rational drug use.

**End-to-end behavior**: A pharmacist views pending prescriptions, reviews the diagnosis and medications, and either approves or rejects the prescription with optional comments. The prescription status updates accordingly, and the patient is notified of the result.

**What this includes**:
- Prescription review endpoint \(pharmacist role\) with approve/reject + comments
- Prescription status state machine: ISSUED → REVIEWING → APPROVED / REJECTED
- Query endpoint for pending prescriptions \(pharmacist view\)
- Prescription detail view with full context \(diagnosis, medications, patient info\)
- Status update notification \(can be simple status field, no real-time push needed for phase 1\)
- Audit trail: reviewer ID, review time, comments

**What this does NOT include**: Real-time notifications \(WebSocket\), drug interaction checking, or automatic rejection rules.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] POST `/api/v1/prescription/{id}/review` accepts action \(APPROVE/REJECT\) + comments
- [ ] Prescription status transitions: ISSUED → REVIEWING → APPROVED / REJECTED
- [ ] Only pharmacists can review \(role-based authorization\)
- [ ] GET `/api/v1/pharmacist/prescriptions/pending` returns prescriptions awaiting review
- [ ] Review records include reviewer ID, timestamp, comments
- [ ] Rejected prescription cannot be approved later \(terminal state\)
- [ ] Patient can see review status and comments in prescription detail

## Blocked by

- #7 \(Prescription Issuance with Inventory Deduction\)
EOF
\)" 2>&1)

Bash(gh issue create --title "Frontend H5 Integration" --body "$\(cat <<'EOF'
## What to build

Complete patient-facing H5 frontend so the entire core flow can be demonstrated end-to-end in a browser for interviews, competitions, or thesis defense.

**End-to-end behavior**: A patient opens the H5 page, registers an account, logs in, uploads a medical image for AI analysis, has a multi-turn AI consultation, grabs an appointment slot via seckill, views their registration order, and checks their prescription history. The UI is clean, modern, and demo-ready.

**What this includes**:
- Vue + Element Plus \(or similar template/low-code framework\)
- Pages:
  - Register / Login
  - Home \(dashboard with quick actions\)
  - Image upload + AI analysis result display
  - AI consultation chat interface \(SSE streaming with typewriter effect\)
  - Schedule list + seckill button
  - My orders \(registration orders with status\)
  - My prescriptions \(list + detail view\)
- API integration with JWT token management \(localStorage\)
- Responsive H5 layout \(mobile-friendly\)
- Clean, modern UI suitable for demo/presentation

**What this does NOT include**: Doctor portal, admin portal, or real-time notifications \(out of scope for phase 1\).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Patient can register and login via H5 pages
- [ ] Patient can upload image and see AI analysis results
- [ ] Patient can chat with AI doctor \(SSE streaming works in browser\)
- [ ] Patient can view schedules and click "grab appointment" \(seckill\)
- [ ] Patient can view registration order status
- [ ] Patient can view prescription list and details
- [ ] JWT token persisted in localStorage, auto-attached to API requests
- [ ] UI is responsive and works on mobile browsers
- [ ] Complete demo flow works: register → login → upload → consult → seckill → view orders → view prescriptions

## Blocked by

- #1 \(Foundation\)
- #2 \(User Registration & JWT Authentication\)
- #3 \(Doctor Schedule Management\)
- #4 \(Seckill Registration with Redis Pre-deduction\)
- #5 \(Multimodal Image Analysis\)
- #6 \(RAG Consultation with SSE Streaming\)
- #7 \(Prescription Issuance with Inventory Deduction\)
- #8 \(Prescription Review Workflow\)
EOF
\)" 2>&1)
